fishing_spots.name: Watauga River — Valle Crucis
fishing_spots.description: One of the most celebrated trophy trout streams in the Southeast. The Valle Crucis section is managed as a catch-and-release trophy fishery, holding large wild brown and rainbow trout. Exceptional year-round fishing with hatches of BWOs, sulphurs, and caddis.
fishing_spots.regulations: Catch-and-release only in trophy section. Artificial lures and flies only. No bait fishing. NC fishing license required.
fishing_spots.best_season: Year-round; spring hatches (Apr–Jun) and fall (Sep–Nov) are peak
fishing_spots.access: Multiple roadside pullouts along NC-105 and SR-1112 through Valle Crucis

access_points.name: Valle Crucis Fly Fishing Access
access_points.description: Primary public wading access for the Valle Crucis trophy section. Deep pools hold large brown trout.
access_points.notes: Catch-and-release only. Artificial flies and lures only. Watch for other anglers.

access_points.name: Mast Farm Inn Pool
access_points.description: Scenic stretch near the historic Mast Farm Inn. Good dry fly water with long flat runs.
access_points.notes: Public access along the road. Respect private property boundaries.

fishing_spots.name: New River — Smallmouth & Musky
fishing_spots.description: The ancient New River — one of the oldest on Earth — offers exceptional smallmouth bass and muskellunge fishing. The lower sections near Sparta and Jefferson hold trophy-class musky. The upper reaches near the state park are excellent for bass on poppers and streamers.
fishing_spots.regulations: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for current musky size and bag limits (typically 36" minimum).
fishing_spots.best_season: Late spring through fall; musky peak in Oct–Nov
fishing_spots.access: New River State Park offers the best public access. Multiple canoe access points along the river.

access_points.name: New River State Park Fishing
access_points.description: Fish from the canoe launch area or wade downstream. Smallmouth bass hold near the rocky shoals. Evening topwater action can be explosive.
access_points.notes: NC fishing license required. Day use fee for state park. Musky: check current size limits.

access_points.name: Tessentee Creek Confluence
access_points.description: Productive musky and smallmouth spot at the creek confluence. Deep pools and slack water off the main current hold fish.
access_points.notes: Best accessed by canoe or kayak. Musky fishing peaks in fall. Use large suckers or musky plugs.

fishing_spots.name: Wilson Creek — Trophy Trout
fishing_spots.description: A wild and scenic designated river managed as a catch-and-release trophy trout fishery. Wilson Creek holds some of the largest wild brown trout in western North Carolina. The gorge section is remote and provides a true wilderness fishing experience.
fishing_spots.regulations: Catch-and-release only. Artificial flies and lures only. NC fishing license required. Trophy section has special regulations — verify boundaries with NCWRC.
fishing_spots.best_season: Spring (Mar–Jun) and fall (Sep–Nov); summer evenings for dry flies
fishing_spots.access: USFS Forest Road 981 runs along the creek. Parking at several pull-offs. Remote — plan accordingly.

access_points.name: Wilson Creek Trophy Section
access_points.description: Prime trophy section managed for wild brown trout. Challenging technical water with large fish. Streamers and nymphs produce year-round.
access_points.notes: Catch-and-release only. Artificial only. Waders recommended — water is cold year-round.

access_points.name: Mortimer Area Trout Water
access_points.description: Upper Wilson Creek near Mortimer Campground. Good stocked rainbow trout water with pocket pools and riffles.
access_points.notes: Some sections are hatchery-supported. Check NCWRC for stocking schedules. Camping available nearby.

fishing_spots.name: Elk Creek — Native Brook Trout
fishing_spots.description: A pristine high-elevation tributary of the Watauga River near Elk Park. The upper reaches are home to native Appalachian brook trout, the only trout species native to western NC. Small stream fishing at its best — short casts, stealthy approach, and beautiful fish.
fishing_spots.regulations: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for any Heritage Trout Water designations — artificial only, special bag limits in some sections.
fishing_spots.best_season: Spring and fall; summer mornings on the upper creek
fishing_spots.access: Roadside access along Elk Creek Road. Some sections require short hikes.

access_points.name: Upper Elk Creek Brook Trout Water
access_points.description: Upper reaches hold native brook trout in classic pocket water. Small dry flies and nymphs work best. Trout are 6–10" but striking in color.
access_points.notes: Check for Heritage Trout Water status — may be artificial only with reduced bag limits.

fishing_spots.name: Linville River — Gorge Trout
fishing_spots.description: Flowing through Linville Gorge Wilderness, the Linville River offers wild trout fishing in one of the most spectacular settings in the Southern Appalachians. Remote hike-in access keeps fishing pressure low and trout sizes up. The river holds wild brown and rainbow trout in its deep gorge pools.
fishing_spots.regulations: NC fishing license required. Check NCWRC for Linville Gorge special regulations — portions may be catch-and-release or artificial only.
fishing_spots.best_season: Spring through fall; summer can be productive in the deep gorge
fishing_spots.access: Hike-in only via Linville Gorge Trail (~3–5 miles one way). Wilderness permit may be required on weekends May–October.

access_points.name: Linville Gorge River Access
access_points.description: Remote wild trout fishing deep in the gorge. Minimal fishing pressure, large wild trout. The hike makes it well worth it.
access_points.notes: Hike-in only, 3–5 miles each way. Weekend wilderness permit required May–Oct. Pack out everything.

fishing_spots.name: South Fork New River
fishing_spots.description: A smaller tributary of the New River system running through Ashe and Alleghany counties. Excellent stocked trout water in the upper reaches and smallmouth bass in the lower sections. More accessible than many High Country streams with good roadside access.
fishing_spots.regulations: NC fishing license required. Standard mountain trout regulations apply in most sections.
fishing_spots.best_season: Spring stocking season (March–May) and fall; summer smallmouth
fishing_spots.access: Multiple roadside access points along US-221 and secondary roads through West Jefferson and Jefferson.

access_points.name: Jefferson Area Trout Access
access_points.description: Regularly stocked trout water near Jefferson. Multiple wading spots along secondary roads. Good family fishing.
access_points.notes: NCWRC stocks rainbow trout March through May. Early morning fishing before crowds arrive.

fishing_spots.name: South Toe River — Mount Mitchell Area
fishing_spots.description: Flowing from the flanks of Mount Mitchell, the South Toe is one of the coldest mountain rivers in the region. The headwaters near Black Mountain Campground offer exceptional native brook trout fishing, while lower sections hold stocked rainbow and brown trout.
fishing_spots.regulations: NC fishing license required. Upper headwater sections near Mount Mitchell may have special regulations — verify with NCWRC.
fishing_spots.best_season: Spring and early summer; the high elevation keeps it fishable even in August
fishing_spots.access: USFS Road 472 and the Black Mountain Campground provide access to upper sections. Lower sections accessible via NC-80.

access_points.name: Black Mountain Campground Brook Trout
access_points.description: Native brook trout in the cold headwaters below Mount Mitchell. Small stream with pocket pools. An Appalachian classic.
access_points.notes: Artificial lures only in designated sections. USFS day-use fee. Very cold water — felt-sole waders recommended.

access_points.name: Toecane River Access — Lower Section
access_points.description: Lower South Toe with regularly stocked rainbow trout. Good wading water with riffles and pools.
access_points.notes: NCWRC stocking schedule available online. Standard mountain trout regulations.

fishing_spots.name: Nolichucky River — Erwin TN
fishing_spots.description: Below the dramatic Nolichucky Gorge, the river opens up near Erwin, TN into excellent smallmouth bass and stocked rainbow trout water. The Tennessee Wildlife Resources Agency regularly stocks trout in accessible sections, and the bass fishing in the lower gorge tailwater is outstanding.
fishing_spots.regulations: Tennessee fishing license required. Check TWRA for stocked trout section boundaries and special regulations.
fishing_spots.best_season: Year-round; trout stocking March–May and fall; bass peak summer
fishing_spots.access: Chestoa Recreation Area in Erwin provides excellent river access with parking and facilities.

access_points.name: Chestoa Recreation Area Fishing
access_points.description: Excellent stocked trout and smallmouth bass water below the gorge. Chestoa has a boat ramp, parking, and river access.
access_points.notes: Tennessee fishing license required. TWRA stocks rainbow trout regularly. Good smallmouth bass water in summer.
